articles.title: Saving tokens in GitHub Copilot
articles.eyebrow: How to reduce costs in 15 minutes
articles.subtitle: From easy wins to advanced context engineering. The goal is not to spend the fewest tokens, but to get maximum value from each one.
articles.summary: Practical techniques for reducing token consumption in agentic coding without losing result quality.

articles.title: How to centrally manage and govern skills for agents
articles.subtitle: Download a central skill, create a local PoC when it needs improvement, send the proposal to GitHub, let agents triage and prepare a PR after human approval, and distribute the improved skill to everyone.
articles.summary: A workflow proposal for team-managed agent skills through a catalog, local PoC, GitHub issue, and agentic triage.

articles.title: Is subscription a suitable business model for an AI product? Is AI getting more expensive? Are tokens getting more expensive? Or are we just moving toward a fair model?
articles.subtitle: Flat subscription is great for adoption, but agents break the natural limits of consumption. I see the future more in a license as an entry ticket, pooled prepaid consumption, and fair top-up units.
articles.summary: A reflection on why agents change the economics of AI products and why a pure flat subscription model runs into limits.

articles.title: AI coding - context vs. measurement, software as memory, I will not learn your software, OpenClaw and the smart home
articles.eyebrow: A home experiment with AI agents
articles.subtitle: A home project with Home Assistant, Copilot CLI, and OpenClaw as a practical lab for thinking about context, feedback, software as memory, and GenUI.
articles.summary: A home experiment showing why context, measurement, feedback, and agentic UX matter for AI coding.

articles.title: Spiral reading: AI podcast from PDFs without losing context
articles.subtitle: How to build a simple workflow in which AI turns a PDF into an audiobook without losing the document's original structure.
articles.summary: A personal workflow for layered reading, listening, and asking AI questions about books, PDFs, and longer texts.

articles.title: Three paths to GenUI and their pros and cons
articles.subtitle: A static artifact, a declarative specification, or fully generated interactive code? Three practical paths to visual GenUI and their trade-offs.
articles.summary: A comparison of three paths to generated UI: static artifacts, declarative specifications, and fully generated interactive code.

articles.title: New Agent Skills Standard for Your Coding Workers in GitHub Copilot
articles.subtitle: How Agent Skills fill the space between AGENTS.md and MCP: context loaded on demand, project instructions and lightweight scripts without the full protocol overhead.
articles.summary: A practical explanation of Agent Skills as the middle ground between AGENTS.md and MCP for coding agents.

articles.title: Coding agents — parallel agent work on your tasks
articles.subtitle: How to use GitHub Copilot CLI, git worktree and background agents in VS Code when you want to run multiple agents locally without them overwriting each other's files.
articles.summary: How to run multiple coding agents in parallel with Copilot CLI, background tasks, and git worktree.

articles.title: Deep dive into AI agent observability with Microsoft Agent Framework - tracing with open-source tools Aspire Dashboard and Langfuse
articles.subtitle: Tracing AI agents via Aspire Dashboard and Langfuse: a quick developer view, anonymization through the collector, and specialized AI observability.

articles.title: Deep dive into observability for AI agents - metrics, logs and dashboards with Azure Monitor, Grafana and Application Insights

articles.subtitle: Metrics, logs, dashboards and backend choices for AI agent observability without unnecessary decoration.
articles.summary: A practical deep dive into AI agent metrics and logs with OpenTelemetry, Prometheus, and Grafana in Azure.

articles.title: Deep dive into AI agent observability with Microsoft Agent Framework - OpenTelemetry semantic conventions and using the OTEL collector
articles.subtitle: OpenTelemetry semantic conventions, Microsoft Agent Framework, and why to put an OTEL Collector between app and backend.
articles.summary: OpenTelemetry semantic conventions for AI agents and the OTEL collector between the app and backends.

articles.title: How to do simple AI text analysis? LLM, fine-tuning, BERT, or embeddings?
articles.subtitle: Using short-text sentiment, I compare quality, cost, latency, and practicality across four approaches.
articles.summary: Comparison of four sentiment analysis paths: LLM prompting, LLM fine-tuning, BERT, and embeddings with logistic regression across quality, cost, latency, and flexibility.

articles.title: Your own data (RAG) for your AI agent, part three - AI chooses the strategy for getting to data itself
articles.eyebrow: The agent chooses a data strategy
articles.subtitle: AI plans reformulations and SQL strategies, fixes errors, and chooses the best path to the data.
articles.summary: How an AI agent plans queries, reformulations, and SQL strategies for retrieval over private data.

articles.title: Your own data (RAG) for your AI agent, part two - knowledge graph and advanced methods

articles.title: Your own data (RAG) for your AI agent, part one - step by step with Python and PostgreSQL
